meaning: vt.刺激...采取行动 激励
examples: They were used as medicines to ward off diseases, and mixed into perfumes, incent.
Incent, incentivise or to give an incentive?
Awkwardly, though, incent has a rival, incentivise.
And it can get really messy when different stakeholders are competing to incent the sales teams.
His budget includes money to incent state governments to offer paid family leave.
